sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 4-year-old's mother, presented with a knee injury sustained while skiing in Vermont last weekend.
She caught her ski when getting off the lift and fell, resulting in a popping sensation in her knee, which has been painful since.
She was unable to bear weight and had to be taken down by the ski patrol.
The pain is located on the outside of the knee, with a sensation that the knee won't hold her weight.
She was able to bend it immediately after the injury but not now, and straightening it causes significant pain.
She denies numbness or tingling in her toes and can feel her toes okay.
She is an active person who chases her two children and has a supportive husband, but he works long hours, leaving her to care for the children alone.
She takes ibuprofen for pain, which provides some relief.
Past medical history: The patient is otherwise generally healthy, with no history of high blood pressure, diabetes, or other significant medical conditions.
She has a family of four, including two young children.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: Ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has edema and an effusion over the right knee.
Palpation of the right lateral knee elicits pain.
There is decreased flexion and extension of the right knee.
The Lachman sign is positive.
The dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses are palpable.
There is no leg edema in the ankle.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has a ruptured anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) in her right knee.
Problem list: Anterior cruciate ligament tear; knee pain; knee effusion.
Plan:
I prescribed ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
I ordered a knee MRI to confirm the suspected ACL tear.
I will put her in a knee brace to provide support.
I will review the MRI results and discuss further treatment options with her, which may include surgery to repair the ACL.
Follow-up: I will see her again soon to discuss the MRI results and plan further treatment.
